name: utilsds
documentation:
# utilsds

Utilsds is a library that includes classes and function used in ds project such as:
- ds_statistics: 
    - test_kruskal_wallis
- transform_data: 
    - PreprocesingData
- data_processing:
    - encode_one_hot
    - convert_numerical_to_categorized
    - scale_train_test
    - resample_X_y
- data_split: 
    - train_test_validation_split
- visualization: 
    - MetricsPlot,
    - Radar,
    - cluster_characteristics,
    - comparison_density,
    - feature_distribution_box,
    - elbow_visualisation,
    - describe_clusters_metrics

Classification classes:
- experiments:
    - VertexExperiments
- classifier:
    - Classifier
- confusion_matrix:
    - ConfusionMatrix
- modeling:
    - Modeling
- hyperopt:
    - Hyperopt

modules.name: utilsds.modeling
documentation: Model class

classes.name: utilsds.modeling.Modeling

methods.name: utilsds.modeling.Modeling.log_vertex_experiments
documentation: Log results of model to Vertex Experiments.

methods.name: utilsds.modeling.Modeling.calculate_hyperopt_best_params
documentation:
Calculate optimal hyperparameters using hyperopt.

Parameters
----------
space : dict
    Hyperparameter search space definition
n_startup_jobs : int
    Number of random initialization trials
hyperopt_iter : int
    Total number of optimization iterations
is_loss_function : bool, default=False
    If True, metric should be minimized instead of maximized

methods.name: utilsds.modeling.Modeling.get_general_metrics
documentation: Get all metrics and combined confusion_matrix.

Modeling, metrics and vertex logging.
Parameters
----------
model : object
    Model class (scikit-learn compatible) that implements fit/predict methods
X_train : pd.DataFrame
    Training features
X_test : pd.DataFrame
    Test features
y_train : pd.DataFrame
    Training labels
y_test : pd.DataFrame
    Test labels
is_binary_class : bool
    If True, treats as binary classification problem
main_metric : str
    Metric name to optimize during hyperparameter tuning
model_params : dict, optional
    Initial hyperparameters for model initialization
proba : float, default=0.5
    Classification threshold for binary problems
own_metrics : dict, optional
    Custom metrics as {'name': callable(y_true, y_pred)}
metrics_average : str, default='binary'
    Averaging method for precision/recall in multiclass case
beta : float, default=2
    Beta value for fbeta_score calculation
fbeta_average : str, default='binary'
    Averaging method for fbeta_score in multiclass case
fbeta_weights : list, default=[0.5, 0.5]
    Class weights for fbeta_score calculation
name_experiment : str, optional
    Name of the experiment in Vertex AI
data_path : str, optional
    Path to data files in DVC
labels : list, optional
    Class labels for confusion matrix
project : str, optional
    Vertex AI project name
location : str, optional
    Vertex AI location
pylint: disable=attribute-defined-outside-init
pylint: disable=raise-missing-from
pylint: disable=invalid-name
pylint: disable=dangerous-default-value
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
pylint: disable=too-many-locals
pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes

modules.name: utilsds.data_processing
documentation: Data Processor

classes.name: utilsds.data_processing.DataProcessor

methods.name: utilsds.data_processing.DataProcessor.encode_one_hot
documentation:
Function to encode category feature with one-hot-encoder. In result the encoded column is dropped.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): Dataframe containing column to encode.
    col_name (str): Name of column to encode.

Returns:
    (pd.DataFrame): Dataframe containing encoded columns.

methods.name: utilsds.data_processing.DataProcessor.__create_intervals
documentation:
Function to create intervals names from given list, so given elements are both ends of interval. For example,
for given [18, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65] it creates
['18_25', '25_35', '35_45', '45_55', '55_65', '65']
Args:
    int_list (list(floats)): List of elements to create intervals from.
Returns:
    list(strings): List of intervals names.

methods.name: utilsds.data_processing.DataProcessor.convert_numerical_to_categorized
documentation:
Function to convert numerical feature to categorized, basing on given bins - intervals. Every bin is interval left side closed and right side opened.
For bins given: [18, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65] it maps values to categorized : ['18_25', '25_35', '35_45', '45_55', '55_65', '65'].

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): Dataframe numerical containing column to convert.
    bins (list(float)): Numbers that represent elements of intervals to convert the numerical feature.
    col_name (string): Name of column to be converted.

Returns:
    (pd.Dataframe): Dataframe containing converted feature.

methods.name: utilsds.data_processing.DataProcessor.scale_train_test
documentation:
Function to scale train and test inputs by given scaler.

Args:
    X_train (pd.DataFrame): Data with all columns to train model.
    X_test (pd.DataFrame): Data with all columns to test model
    scaler_object (any_scaler): Object of selected scaler to execute.
    params (dict): Dictionary of params for selected scaler.

Returns:
    tuple (pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame): Scaled data for training and testing.

methods.name: utilsds.data_processing.DataProcessor.resample_X_y
documentation:
Function for resampling train data and target column.

Args:
    X_train (pd.DataFrame): Data with all columns to train model.
    y_train (pd.Series): Target column.
    sampler_object (any_sampler): Object of selected sampler to execute.
    params (dict): Dictionary of params for selected sampler.

Returns:
    tuple(pd.DataFrame, pd.Series): Sampled data and target column.

methods.name: utilsds.data_processing.DataProcessor.get_transformed_data
documentation:
Use DataTransformer to transform input data.

Args:
    data (pd.DataFrame): Data to be transformed.
    sqrt_col (Optional[List[str]]): Columns to perform sqrt_col transformation. Defaults to None.
    log_default (Optional[List[str]]): Columns to perform log_default transformation. Defaults to None.
    ihs (Optional[List[str]]): Columns to perform ihs transformation. Defaults to None.
    extensive_log (Optional[List[str]]): Columns to perform extensive_log transformation. Defaults to None.
    neglog (Optional[List[str]]): Columns to perform neglog transformation. Defaults to None.
    boxcox_zero (Optional[List[str]]): Columns to perform boxcox_zero transformation. Defaults to None.
    log_x_divide_2 (Optional[List[str]]): Columns to perform log_x_divide_2 transformation. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: DataFrame with transformed columns.
Class for processing data before training.

modules.name: utilsds.data_split
documentation: Train test validation split function

functions.name: utilsds.data_split.train_test_validation_split
documentation:
Splits a Pandas dataframe into three subsets (train, val, and test).
Function uses train_test_split (from sklearn) and stratify to receive
the same ratio response (y, target) in each splits.
If col_order is given, split is not shuffled, but divided by col_order order (eg. to train, test and validate on separate time spans).

Parameters
----------
data: pd.DataFrame
    Data to split
col_target: str
    Name of column target
train_percent: float (0,1)
    Percent of train data
validate_percent: float (0,1)
    Percent of validate data
test_percent: float (0,1)
    Percent of test data
random_state: int, optional
    Random state for reproducibility. Defaults to 2024.
col_order: string, optional. Defaults to None.
    Column to sort values for train/test/validation split by date. If none, standard split is done - data are shuffled.

WARNING:
    Sum of train_percent, validate_percent and test_percent have to be equal 1.0.

Returns
    X_train, X_test, X_val : Dataframes containing the three splits.
    y_train, y_test, y_val : Series with target variables

methods.name: utilsds.hyperopt.Hyperopt.calculate_hyperopt_best_params
documentation:
Calculate models and return the best parameters in variable self.best_params.

Parameters
----------

    Dict of parameter space, example: "'C': hp.uniform('C', 0.1, 100)"
n_startup_jobs : int, optional
    Number of random hyperparameters search, by default 5

    Number of iteration for hyperopt, by default 100

----------

    Classifier object to optimize
metricser : Metrics
    Metrics object that contains main_metric to be optimized
is_loss_function : bool
    Defines if main_metric is loss_function or needs to be adjusted as loss function

documentation:
Statistical Kruskal-Wallis test.

Parameters

modules.name: utilsds.experiments
documentation: Vertex Experiments

documentation: Calculate and write confusion matrix in vertex experiment.

methods.name: utilsds.experiments.VertexExperiment.log_experiment_results_to_vertex
documentation:
The function saves all values (params, metrics) on Vertex experiments.
Returns
-------
None

Class for logging results of experiments on Vertex Experiments.
Parameters:
    name_experiment : str
        Name of the Vertex Experiments instance.
    model_name : str
        Type of the evaluated model.
    metrics : dict
        Dictionary containing calculated metrics.
    confusion_matrix : numpy.ndarray
        Raw confusion matrix for logging.
    model_params : dict
        Model hyperparameters for logging.
    data_path : str
        Directory path for data in DVC.
    labels : numpy.ndarray
        Array of labels for confusion matrix.
    project : str, optional
        Google Cloud project name (default: "sts-notebooks").
    location : str, optional
        Google Cloud location (default: "europe-west4").

methods.name: utilsds.transform_data.DataTransformer.func_transform_data
documentation:
Function to transform all data.

Returns
-------
pd.Dataframe
    Transformed dataframe.

A class for preprocessing numerical data with various transformation methods.

Parameters
----------
data : pd.DataFrame
    DataFrame containing data to be transformed
sqrt_col : list, optional
    Columns for square root transformation (requires non-negative values)
log_default : list, optional
    Columns for logarithmic transformation with added constant 0.01
ihs : list, optional
    Columns for inverse hyperbolic sine transformation
extensive_log : list, optional
    Columns for extensive logarithmic transformation
neglog : list, optional
    Columns for logarithmic transformation with negative values handling
boxcox_zero : list, optional
    Columns for Box-Cox transformation with zero handling
log_x_divide_2 : list, optional
    Columns for logarithmic transformation with half minimum non-zero value

documentation:
Calculate average f beta score from StratifiedKfold

Parameters
----------
n_splits: int, default=5
    Number of folds. Must be at least 2.

Returns
-------
None

Calculates metrics for model and allows own metrics to be calculated.
Parameters
----------
classifier : object
    Classifier object with fit_predict methods.
main_metric : str
    Name of the main metric used for optimization (e.g., 'accuracy_score', 'fbeta_score')
metrics_average : str, optional (default='binary')
    Averaging method for metrics in multiclass classification.
beta : float, optional (default=2)
    Beta parameter for fbeta_score metric.
fbeta_average : str, optional (default='binary')
    Averaging method for fbeta_score in multiclass classification.
fbeta_weights : list, optional (default=[0.5, 0.5])
    Weights for individual classes when calculating fbeta_score.
own_metrics : dict, optional (default=None)
    Dictionary of custom metrics in format {'name': function(y_test, y_pred)}.
pylint: disable=dangerous-default-value, too-many-instance-attributes, too-many-arguments, bare-except, line-too-long, inconsistent-return-statements, consider-using-enumerate, no-else-return
